sections.title: Overview
details: Equals Equals was a hackathon for underrespresented minorities in tech.
I also realized that almost every demographic group on campus had a hackathon tailed to them (men, women, asian people, jewish people
first one had hapened in 2018
focused on learning, and wanted to encourage newcomers
lead a team of 6 people

wanted to focus on marketing to underrperestned students, mostly black and latinx. When I spoke about other hackathons to black and latinx students at my school they had never heard of them. And I realized that they were not being considered in the marketign strategy of these hackathons.
saw a problem where many people didn't want to attend a hackathon because they were intimidated
made sure to plan workshops all day so that people who wanted to learn all day could do that

sections.title: Hackathon
details: sponsored by css as well
funding from google
funding from see
raised $2-3k
got over 100 students in a room to learn about tech
I taught a workshop on the twitter API and walked a class through creating a basic twitter bot
not as many black people as expected, but still a good event. People don't know was "underresprested minority means" and thought it meant just all minorities
also learned that having workshops attracted more non-men to tech events

sections.title: Subsequent Years
details: I wasn't part of this, but members of the exec team and some volunteers came together to
they were able to engage even more students in 2019
It was a beaudtiful event for the years it existed, but after the 2020 covid pandemic
learned a lot about leadership, event planning, and tailoring events to certain demographic markets
became and informal diversity consultant on campus (lol)
